item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Interesting read, enjoyed at just past midnight. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This never really coalesced into something interesting to me. The bodies appearing seems odd and unexplained and never particularly connected with the monster, which was rather generic. I mean, seriously, glowing eyes and radiation? The poems/religious imagery l and expanding score felt like they were telling me I should take the object more seriously without feeling more than tenuously reflected in the object and its story.

-1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's the exact same anomaly. It's not that there are multiple, it's just the same anomaly in a location where there's…more room for it to show what it really is.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Interesting add-on to the anomalous landscape of China. The blackboxes in Addendum [3418-002] are rather annoying for reading, IMO.

As for the ending, it seems to be a case of 'there is more of this anomalous things out there'. I do hope that it is not entirely the case here with this article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
To see more articles in the Children of the Torch (Huoju zhi Zi) series:

SCP-1428 - Jinwu
SCP-2814 - Heretic of the Torch
SCP-2995 - Radiant Son

For the Xia Anomalous Culture Group series:

SCP-2481 - Kill the Suns
SCP-2841 - Revenge of the Snakes
